beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Phase one uses Claude as an interviewer to force a written spec before any code exists. Phase two generates four divergent static mockups so you explore directions before committing to one. Phase three defines machine-readable DOM contracts that a test runner checks with bun run verify. Each phase hands a different cognitive job to the AI while you stay at every decision point.

props.command: Anthropic builds products with Claude. They published the workflow. Here it is, taken apart.

The three-phase split clicked for me — AI as interviewer, then divergence engine, then verifier — and I especially like that phase three turns design into a passing-or-failing test suite via bun run verify. I want to try this on a small note-taking app. Can you (1) run the phase-one interview and produce a short product spec for me; (2) propose four genuinely divergent phase-two directions with the design bet each one makes; (3) write one DOM-contract fixture I could actually verify?

beats.narration_text: Phase one: no mockups, no code. Just prompts. Claude interviews you about the product — who uses it, what they're trying to do, what would make them trust it. The output is a product spec. The constraint is intentional: if you jump to visuals too early, you anchor on the first thing you see. Phase one forces you to think before you build.

Phase two: four divergent directions. Not one design — four. Static HTML mockups, each taking a different design bet. Minimal versus rich. Data-dense versus narrative. The point is divergence before convergence. Most teams pick one direction and iterate. Anthropic forces four directions and then chooses. The cost is one afternoon. The benefit is you don't spend a week refining the wrong direction.

Phase three: component fixtures and a DOM contract. Every component gets a fixture — a known state it must render correctly. Every contract is machine-readable. You run bun run verify and you get passing or failing component contracts, not a code review. The output isn't a design document. It's a test suite that proves the architecture is sound before any real code ships.

props.sparkLine: The test suite is the output.
The test suite is the output.

beats.narration_text: The three-phase structure is not about using AI more. It's about using it at the right points. Phase one: AI as interviewer, forcing clarity before commitment. Phase two: AI as divergence engine, generating four directions you wouldn't have had time to explore. Phase three: AI as verifier, catching architectural gaps before they become technical debt. Each phase delegates a different cognitive job to Claude. You stay in the loop at every decision point.

props.artifactTitle: How We Claude Code — the pattern
artifactLines: Phase 1: Interview → spec. AI as interviewer. Forces clarity before commitment.
Phase 2: Spec → four mockups. AI as divergence engine. Four bets, one afternoon.
Phase 3: Mockups → DOM contracts. AI as verifier. Architectural gaps caught before shipping.
The constraint: no mockups in phase 1, no code in phase 2, no vibe-checks in phase 3.
The output at every phase is a decision artifact, not a conversation.
props.sparkLine: Delegate the cognitive job. Keep the decision.
